text.title: eu-ai-act-compliance Decision Tree
text.summary: Risk-tier routing from a system description.

decision-tree.root-question: Does the system use any prohibited practice from Article 5 (social scoring, manipulative subliminal techniques, real-time biometric ID in public spaces, emotion recognition in workplace/education)?

branch.conclusion: risk_tier=unacceptable. HALT — system cannot be deployed in EU.

branch.question: Does any feature fall under Annex III (biometrics, critical infrastructure, education/vocational training, employment/HR, essential services like credit, law enforcement, migration, justice/democracy)?

branch.conclusion: risk_tier=high. Run full pipeline: conformity assessment, model card, bias report, explainability, human review, EU database registration.

branch.question: Does Article 50 transparency apply? (Chatbot, emotion recognition, biometric categorisation, deepfake content, AI-generated text on matters of public interest.)

branch.conclusion: risk_tier=limited. Article 50 disclosure copy + machine-readable watermarks where required.
risk_tier=minimal. No formal obligation. Re-classify if features change.

text.example: q-prohibited → branch(false) → q-annex-iii → branch(true) → conclusion(r2-human-legal-gate). Result: full high-risk pipeline.
